denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 04/07/2026
Aetna Choice® POS II (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W102486566          Group #: GRP-1314
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-147039
Claim #: CLM-2026-204643
Date of Service (requested): 03/18/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Central States Medical Associates

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 03/20/2026 for Ocrelizumab infusion related to Relapsing multiple sclerosis (G35). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Ocrelizumab infusion
Diagnosis (as submitted): Relapsing multiple sclerosis (G35)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied:
Clinical criteria applied: InterQual criteria (2018) and/or older MCG modules (superseded guideline reference). CPB 0134 under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) and InterQual® acute-care criteria

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CPB 0134 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Ocrelizumab infusion is not considered evidence-based for Relapsing multiple sclerosis (G35) under current medical policy. Published criteria do not support authorization.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the MCG Care Guidelines (edition not specified) and MCG Care Guidelines (edition not specified) and utilization-management requirements associated with not evidence based. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. This review used clinical criteria from our designated utilization-management program; the criteria document is available upon request.

Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with an Aetna medical director before filing a written appeal.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W102486566) and reference UM-2026-147039. 

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
Utilization Management Department
clinical_context: The 42-year-old member has Relapsing multiple sclerosis (G35). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Ocrelizumab infusion recommended as the next appropriate step. Two clinical relapses 2024–2025; MRI new T2 lesions despite teriflunomide. Submitted documentation supports medical necessity for Ocrelizumab infusion in the setting of Relapsing multiple sclerosis (G35); the denial letter does not discuss those records or cite the objective findings in the file.
